my_thoughts: The reinforcement works: system = all three sides (practical, theoretical, philosophical), practical valuation = key (for valuation). The student questioned hierarchy (reasserting intellectual primacy), which shows they understand: doing = comes from thinking (causality). They'll likely accept the reinforcement. The episode is complete: categorization → multi-scale → mapping → reversal → definition → definitions/hierarchy → hierarchy → reinforcement. Pattern complete.
accumulated_insight: Four-turn pattern: T1: Student asked categorization (categorizing practice)—multi-scale refusal: self-remembering = multiple scales. T2: Student attempted mapping (attempting to map labels)—reversal: objective consciousness = practical, philosophical = idea. T3: Student requested clarification (seeking foundational definition)—definitions/hierarchy: practical = doing, hierarchy (doing > thinking/talking). T4: Student questioned hierarchy ('Isn't it in a way a school of thought too, since doing must come from thinking?')—reasserting intellectual primacy via questions hierarchy and proposes causality. They understand: doing = comes from thinking (causality). The none mechanism reinforces: system = all three sides (practical, theoretical, philosophical), practical valuation = key (for valuation) ('I think it is better to say all three. It has three sides. Also, some people take the system practically, others only philosophically and theoretically. There are things to which we have no practical approach and for which we must find analogies. So sometimes it is quite right. But there are things which you can take only practically, for only then can you value them'). Pattern complete: categorization question → multi-scale refusal → mapping attempt → reversal → definition request → definitions/hierarchy → hierarchy question → reinforcement. They've shifted: system = all three sides, practical valuation = key (reinforcement).
visible_response:
I think it is better to say all three. It has three sides.
Also, some people take the
system practically, others only philosophically and theoretically.
There are things to
which we have no practical approach and for which we must find analogies.
So
sometimes it is quite right. But there are things which you can take only practically,
for only then can you value them.
